assertions.seq_strictly_monotonic_within_single_DO_: PASS for msg#1 batch (1,2,3) and msg#3 batch (4,5,6); seq reset observed between msg#1 and msg#2

finding.severity: moderate (dev-env confirmed; production behavior requires confirmation)
finding.description: SessionDO.messageSeq is declared as `private messageSeq = 0` (apps/orchestrator/src/agents/session-do.ts:113) — an in-memory class field with no persistence to `this.storage`. When the DO instance is evicted and re-hydrated (observable in miniflare dev after ~25s idle between turns), the counter resets to 0. The client's wire handler (apps/orchestrator/src/features/agent-orch/use-coding-agent.ts:272) silently drops frames where `seq <= lastSeq` — so post-reset frames bearing seq values the client has already seen are discarded WITHOUT triggering the gap-snapshot recovery path (`onGap` only fires when `seq > lastSeq + 1`).
finding.observedImpact: Across three user turns (ONE / THREE / FOUR) over a single live WebSocket (same CDP requestId, no reconnect event), the seq stream was 0,1,2,3 → 1,2,3 → 4,5,6. Client's lastSeq after the first batch = 3. The second batch's seq=1,2,3 frames (carrying the `usr-client-f31526e4-…` user-echo and `msg-5` assistant reply) were silently dropped by the client. The UI's REST fallback (`/api/sessions/:id/messages`) and the DO-side history persistence kept server state consistent (10 turns, 889 ctx tokens), so the bug is latent until a client needs the dropped rows (e.g., cold tab, rehydrate from persist, branch-nav).
repro: Create a session, send one message, wait for reply.
Wait ~25s (enough for miniflare to evict the idle DO instance).
Send another message. Capture WS frames via CDP Network.webSocketFrameReceived.
Observe: `seq` resets to 1 on the next broadcast post-rehydrate despite the client's lastSeq ≥ 1.
finding.suggestedFix: Persist messageSeq to DO storage (`ctx.storage.put('messageSeq', this.messageSeq)` on every advance; rehydrate on `constructor`/`blockConcurrencyWhile`). Alternatively, always emit a `{kind:'snapshot', reason:'reconnect'}` to a reconnecting client on DO rehydrate so the client resets its `lastSeq` via `Math.max(lastSeq, frame.payload.version)`.
finding.reproducibility: Confirmed in this verify session; raw frames at `.kata/verification-evidence/vp1-ws-frames-14.ndjson`.
finding.productionRelevance: Cloudflare DOs evict on worker redeploy, memory pressure, and cold starts in normal production. The reset should be verifiable on staging; unit tests (697/697) do not cover the DO-rehydrate path because they exercise broadcast within a single in-memory instance.

evidence.onConnectSnapshot: seq=0 kind=snapshot reason=reconnect carried prior msg-3 (TWO) from pre-reload DO state — confirms the DO-pushed snapshot-on-connect hydrates the client.
evidence.restFallback: GET /api/sessions/<id>/messages returned HTTP 200 with body `{messages: {messages: [...]}}` — endpoint exists; body shape nested one level deeper than VP2 step 4 assumes, may want confirmation that `queryCollectionOptions` unwraps correctly.

evidence.clientProposedIdsEchoed: All three `usr-client-<uuid>` ids sent by the client came back from the DO verbatim in delta frames, matching the B5/B6 'server accepts client id as primary id' design.
evidence.renderCountAssertion: Not verified — would require React-Profiler init-script; not in scope of this tap.

vpResults.title: Rewind and resubmit broadcast snapshots (B2)
vpResults.reason: Rewind/resubmit UI actions not exercised in this run. Tap is now proven; a follow-up session can click rewind and resubmit and capture the expected `reason:'rewind'|'resubmit'` snapshot frames.

evidence.branchInfoOnInitialSnapshot: Initial snapshot carried `branchInfo: []` — field present, confirming the piggyback channel wired per spec.

vpResults.title: Full integration smoke (B1–B11)
vpResults.evidence: Session create → 3 user turns → server-assigned assistant ids → ctx tokens advance → num_turns=10 → UI remains Connected/idle. Rewind/resubmit/branch-nav + reload steps not exercised.

findings: messageSeq non-persistence under DO rehydrate → silent client-side frame drops. Not a spec regression (PR spec did not mandate persistence) but a correctness gap the seq-based wire protocol should close. Recommend filing as a follow-up issue gated on staging repro.
